data.Title: Respond to Citation
data.Description: Record a driver's response (pay or request hearing), advancing the citation from Issued to Responded.

data.Title: Request Hearing
data.Description: Create a hearing request for a citation and advance the contest track to HearingRequested.

data.Title: Schedule Hearing
data.Description: Manager schedules a requested hearing (HearingRequested → Scheduled).

data.Title: Record Hearing Outcome
data.Description: Record a hearing outcome (Dismissed/Upheld), driving liability and the payment track.

data.Title: Pay Citation
data.Description: Take an online payment in full and close the citation (Due → Paid).

data.Title: Recompute Jurisdiction Cases
data.Description: Force re-derivation of all citations under a jurisdiction after a rule change (normally automatic via the view).

data.Title: Work Queue Report
data.Description: Return the prioritized outstanding-work list, bucketed by urgency.

data.Title: Ask the Assistant
data.Description: Submit a question; classify it, answer it, and persist a fully-costed AssistantTurn.

data.Title: Nightly Collections Sweep
data.Description: Scheduled job that moves Late citations past the collections window into Collections.

data.Title: Open a contest
data.Description: Opens a contest on a Citation, capturing the selected contest grounds and transitioning the contest-track to its initial contested state; writes a CaseEvents entry.

data.Title: Withdraw a contest
data.Description: Withdraws an open contest, returning the Citation to the pay-or-contest decision point and logging the withdrawal.

data.Title: Reschedule a hearing
data.Description: Reschedules a Hearing to a new date/time, checking calendar conflicts and writing the change to CaseEvents.

data.Title: Mark hearing prepped
data.Description: Marks a Hearing as prepared (evidence and grounds assembled), advancing the contest-track readiness state.

data.Title: Issue a determination
data.Description: Records the Hearing determination (upheld/dismissed/reduced) on the Citation, advancing the citation-lifecycle to adjudicated and triggering fee/point consequences.

data.Title: Generate determination letter
data.Description: Generates the formal determination letter from the jurisdiction template and attaches it to the Citation.

data.Title: Dismiss a citation
data.Description: Dismisses a Citation (e.g. successful contest), zeroing the amount due, reversing any assessed points, and closing the lifecycle.

data.Title: Refund a payment
data.Description: Reverses a Payment when a paid Citation is later dismissed, restoring make-whole state and removing points; barred from external-llm.

data.Title: Enroll in a payment plan
data.Description: Enrolls an eligible Driver in an installment plan, generating the schedule of Payments and checking JurisdictionRules eligibility.

data.Title: Record an installment
data.Description: Records an installment payment against a plan, advancing the payment-track and accruing a late penalty if past due.

data.Title: Accrue late penalties
data.Description: Scheduled job that accrues late penalties on overdue Payments per JurisdictionRules and flags them in the work queue.

data.Title: Assess license points
data.Description: Assesses license points for an adjudicated Citation per the jurisdiction point schedule and rolls them up to the Driver total.

data.Title: Recompute driver points
data.Description: Recomputes a Driver's total license points across all adjudicated Citations, used after a dismissal reverses points.

data.Title: Check suspension threshold
data.Description: Reports whether a Driver has crossed a jurisdiction suspension threshold based on accumulated points.

data.Title: Bulk import citations
data.Description: Validates and imports a batch of Citations from a jurisdiction feed, reporting per-row conflicts before commit.

data.Title: OCR a citation document
data.Description: Runs OCR on an uploaded citation image to extract structured fields, writing cost telemetry for the job.

data.Title: Driver self-service lookup
data.Description: Public lookup that returns a Driver's own Citation by number and last name for the self-service portal, redaction-scoped.

data.Title: Advance citation state
data.Description: Manually advances a Citation to a valid next lifecycle state via the state-machine backbone, with guard enforcement and CaseEvents logging.

data.Title: Estimate OCR cost
data.Description: Projects the per-document OCR cost from page count and current model pricing before a job runs.

data.Title: Cost rollup report
data.Description: Aggregates assistant and OCR spend by model, role, jurisdiction, and day for the cost dashboard.

data.Title: Deadline report
data.Description: Lists every Citation, contest, and Payment approaching or past its deadline, sorted by urgency.

data.Title: Case timeline
data.Description: Returns the chronological CaseEvents history for a Citation for the timeline view.

data.Title: SAVE to rulebook
data.Description: Captures live config-table edits back into the rulebook JSON and triggers a rebuild; surfaces a diff before writing.

data.Title: Drift check
data.Description: Reports rows where live DB config has diverged from the rulebook, the interlock on the SAVE/rebuild cycle.

data.Title: Toggle a package
data.Description: Enables or disables a feature package, adding or removing its screens, tables, and endpoints from the platform.

data.Title: Toggle feature license
data.Description: Toggles entitlement of an individual feature, honored by gating, the sidebar, and the unlicensed-reveal surface.

data.Title: Impersonate a role
data.Description: Starts a view-as-role session so an admin sees the exact rows, fields, and redactions a chosen role experiences.

schema.Description: Kebab-cased PK derived from the citation number (e.g. 'TC-2026-0001' -> 'tc-2026-0001').

schema.name: IsMockData
schema.Description: TRUE for seed/demo rows; distinguishes mock data from real records.

schema.Description: Human citation/ticket number printed on the ticket.

schema.Description: FK to the cited Driver.

schema.Description: Display name of the cited driver.

schema.name: ViolationType
schema.Description: FK to the cited ViolationType.

schema.Description: Description of the cited violation.

schema.name: Jurisdiction
schema.Description: FK to the Jurisdiction in which the citation was issued (governs deadlines, penalties, points).

schema.name: JurisdictionLabel
schema.Description: Display name of the issuing jurisdiction.

schema.name: BaseFineUsd
schema.Description: Base fine for the cited violation, pulled from the ViolationType.

schema.name: ViolationPoints
schema.Description: License points for the cited violation, pulled from the ViolationType.

schema.name: IssuedOn
schema.Description: Date the citation was issued to the driver.

schema.name: AsOfDate
schema.Description: The 'today' reference date used to evaluate deadlines for this citation (lets seed data exercise overdue/late states deterministically).

schema.name: RespondedOn
schema.Description: Date the driver responded (paid or requested a hearing). Null if no response yet.

schema.name: ContestRequested
schema.Description: Whether the driver elected to contest the citation (request a hearing) rather than pay.

schema.name: PaidOn
schema.Description: Date the fine was paid in full. Null if not yet paid.

schema.name: AmountPaidUsd
schema.Description: Total amount the driver has paid against this citation. Null/0 if nothing paid.

schema.name: DaysToRespond
schema.Description: Response window in days, pulled from the issuing jurisdiction.

schema.name: DaysToPayAfterRuling
schema.Description: Days-to-pay window, pulled from the issuing jurisdiction.

schema.name: LatePenaltyPct
schema.Description: Late-penalty percentage, pulled from the issuing jurisdiction.

schema.name: DaysLateToCollections
schema.Description: Days-late-to-collections window, pulled from the issuing jurisdiction.

schema.name: ResponseDueDate
schema.Description: Deadline to respond: IssuedOn + the jurisdiction's response window.

schema.name: DaysUntilResponseDue
schema.Description: Days remaining (negative if overdue) until the response deadline, measured from AsOfDate.

schema.name: IsResponseOverdue
schema.Description: True when no response was filed and the response deadline has passed as of AsOfDate.

schema.name: CountOfHearings
schema.Description: Number of hearing records attached to this citation.

schema.name: LatestHearingOutcome
schema.Description: Outcome of the most recent hearing for this citation (Pending if a hearing exists but has no outcome; blank if no hearing).

schema.name: ContestStatus
schema.Description: Contest/Hearing state machine: NotContested when the driver did not elect to contest; otherwise HearingRequested -> Scheduled -> Heard, reflected from the latest hearing's outcome.

schema.name: IsDismissed
schema.Description: True when the latest hearing outcome dismissed the citation.

schema.name: IsGuilty
schema.Description: True when the driver is liable: either found guilty/upheld at hearing, or defaulted by missing the response deadline without contesting.

schema.name: AmountDueUsd
schema.Description: Amount currently owed: 0 if dismissed; otherwise the base fine plus the jurisdiction's late penalty if the payment is late.

schema.name: PaymentDueDate
schema.Description: Date the fine is due: the later anchor (response deadline) plus the jurisdiction's days-to-pay window.

schema.name: IsPaymentLate
schema.Description: True when the driver is liable, has not paid in full, and the payment due date has passed as of AsOfDate.

schema.name: IsInCollections
schema.Description: True when a late payment has remained unpaid past the jurisdiction's collections window.

schema.name: PaymentStatus
schema.Description: Payment/Penalty state machine: NotOwed (dismissed) -> Paid -> Collections -> Late -> Due. Evaluated in priority order.

schema.name: EffectivePoints
schema.Description: License points this citation actually contributes to the driver: the violation's points if the driver is liable and not dismissed, otherwise 0. Drives the driver's ActivePoints rollup.

schema.name: CitationStatus
schema.Description: Citation lifecycle state machine: Issued -> Responded -> InContest -> Adjudicated -> Closed. The top-level status synthesizing the other tracks.

schema.name: Hearings
schema.Description: Reverse FK: hearing records for this citation.

schema.name: Payments

schema.Description: Reverse FK: append-only case-event log entries for this citation.

data.StringValue: A traffic ticket everyone understands, modeled as four state machines and a multi-jurisdiction rules engine.

A driver gets a citation. They can pay it or contest it at a hearing. Miss the deadline and you default; lose the hearing and you owe; pay late and a jurisdiction-specific penalty applies; rack up points and your license is warned then suspended. Every deadline, fine, penalty, point cap, and threshold is a row in the Jurisdictions table — change a jurisdiction's rule and every citation under it re-derives. The four state machines (Citation, Contest, Payment, License) are calculated fields, never stored-and-mutated.

data.JsonValue: ["Show how a familiar, deadline-driven case flow decomposes into calculated state machines","Demonstrate a multi-jurisdiction rules engine where regulations are data, not code","Illustrate an append-only event log coexisting with computed current state"]

["Citation: Issued -> Responded -> InContest -> Adjudicated -> Closed","Contest: NotContested | HearingRequested -> Scheduled -> Heard","Payment: Pending -> Due -> Late -> Collections | Paid | NotOwed","License: Valid -> Warning -> Suspended"]

data.StringValue: Courthouse-meets-control-panel: a citizen-facing contest portal sitting on a fully self-describing rules-and-lifecycle engine.
Slate navy, signal amber, verdict green, penalty red.

data.JsonValue: [{"label": "Features", "value": "90+ fully-specified capabilities, 15 starred"}, {"label": "Business Rules", "value": "57 numbered TT-* rules, every feature ref resolves"}, {"label": "State Machines", "value": "4 lifecycles, 25 states, 26 guarded transitions"}, {"label": "Conformance", "value": "194 test cases / 351 expectations, 100% coverage"}]

data.StringValue: Built rulebook-first across ten Leopold loops: features, rules, navigation, endpoints, state machines, a test surface, screen hints, deeper domain data, and meta — every layer cross-linked and consistency-gated before any app code.

The traffic-ticket-contest rulebook is a fully-specified, buildable platform spec: 90+ features each with SourceText + RuleRefs + a nav route + tests; 57 business rules; 36 API actions; 4 complete state machines; 194 conformance test cases; 525+ screen/field display hints; all FKs and refs consistency-gated.

data.Title: Transition: citation-lifecycle--issued->responded
data.Description: Guard 'Driver responds (pays or requests hearing).' allows citation-lifecycle--issued -> citation-lifecycle--responded for role representative.

data.Title: Transition: citation-lifecycle--responded->incontest
data.Description: Guard 'Driver elects to contest and a hearing exists.' allows citation-lifecycle--responded -> citation-lifecycle--incontest for role representative.

data.Title: Transition: citation-lifecycle--issued->adjudicated
data.Description: Guard 'Response deadline passes with no response (default).' allows citation-lifecycle--issued -> citation-lifecycle--adjudicated for role system.

data.Title: Transition: citation-lifecycle--incontest->adjudicated
data.Description: Guard 'Hearing returns a guilty/upheld outcome.' allows citation-lifecycle--incontest -> citation-lifecycle--adjudicated for role system.

data.Title: Transition: citation-lifecycle--adjudicated->closed
data.Description: Guard 'Fine paid in full or citation dismissed.' allows citation-lifecycle--adjudicated -> citation-lifecycle--closed for role system.

data.Title: Transition: contest-track--notcontested->hearingrequested
data.Description: Guard 'Driver requests a hearing.' allows contest-track--notcontested -> contest-track--hearingrequested for role representative.

data.Title: Transition: contest-track--hearingrequested->scheduled
data.Description: Guard 'Court schedules the hearing.' allows contest-track--hearingrequested -> contest-track--scheduled for role manager.

data.Title: Transition: contest-track--scheduled->heard
data.Description: Guard 'Hearing is held and an outcome recorded.' allows contest-track--scheduled -> contest-track--heard for role manager.

data.Title: Transition: payment-track--pending->due
data.Description: Guard 'Driver found liable; fine assessed.' allows payment-track--pending -> payment-track--due for role system.

data.Title: Transition: payment-track--due->paid
data.Description: Guard 'Driver pays in full.' allows payment-track--due -> payment-track--paid for role representative.

data.Title: Transition: payment-track--due->late
data.Description: Guard 'Payment due date passes unpaid.' allows payment-track--due -> payment-track--late for role system.

data.Title: Transition: payment-track--late->collections
data.Description: Guard 'Late beyond the jurisdiction's collections window.' allows payment-track--late -> payment-track--collections for role system.

data.Title: Transition: payment-track--pending->notowed
data.Description: Guard 'Citation dismissed at hearing.' allows payment-track--pending -> payment-track--notowed for role system.

data.Title: Transition: license-track--valid->warning
data.Description: Guard 'Active points reach the warning threshold.' allows license-track--valid -> license-track--warning for role system.

data.Title: Transition: license-track--warning->suspended
data.Description: Guard 'Active points reach the suspension threshold.' allows license-track--warning -> license-track--suspended for role system.

data.Title: Transition: citation-lifecycle--incontest->dismissed
data.Description: Guard 'Contest succeeds; citation dismissed and points reversed.' allows citation-lifecycle--incontest -> citation-lifecycle--dismissed for role manager.

data.Title: Transition: contest-track--hearingrequested->withdrawn
data.Description: Guard 'Representative withdraws the contest before the hearing.' allows contest-track--hearingrequested -> contest-track--withdrawn for role representative.

data.Title: Transition: contest-track--scheduled->withdrawn
data.Description: Guard 'Contest withdrawn after scheduling but before hearing.' allows contest-track--scheduled -> contest-track--withdrawn for role representative.

data.Title: Transition: contest-track--heard->upheld
data.Description: Guard 'Hearing determination upholds the citation in full.' allows contest-track--heard -> contest-track--upheld for role manager.

data.Title: Transition: contest-track--heard->dismissed
data.Description: Guard 'Hearing determination dismisses the citation.' allows contest-track--heard -> contest-track--dismissed for role manager.

data.Title: Transition: contest-track--heard->reduced
data.Description: Guard 'Hearing determination reduces the violation or fine.' allows contest-track--heard -> contest-track--reduced for role manager.

data.Title: Transition: payment-track--collections->paid
data.Description: Guard 'Driver settles the outstanding balance in collections.' allows payment-track--collections -> payment-track--paid for role representative.

data.Title: Transition: payment-track--paid->notowed
data.Description: Guard 'Citation dismissed after payment; refund issued.' allows payment-track--paid -> payment-track--notowed for role manager.

data.Title: Transition: license-track--valid->pointsassessed
data.Description: Guard 'Adjudicated citation assesses license points.' allows license-track--valid -> license-track--pointsassessed for role manager.

data.Title: Transition: license-track--pointsassessed->warning
data.Description: Guard 'Accumulated points cross the warning threshold.' allows license-track--pointsassessed -> license-track--warning for role manager.

data.Title: Transition: license-track--suspended->reinstated
data.Description: Guard 'Points reduced (dismissal/time) below threshold; license reinstated.' allows license-track--suspended -> license-track--reinstated for role admin.

data.Title: Rule enforced: R1 — Citation requires driver, violation and jurisdiction
data.Description: A citation must reference exactly one driver, one violation type, and one issuing jurisdiction.

data.Title: Rule enforced: R2 — Response due date is derived from the jurisdiction
data.Description: ResponseDueDate = IssuedOn + the issuing jurisdiction's DaysToRespond. Changing the jurisdiction's rule re-derives every citation under it.

data.Title: Rule enforced: R3 — A response is pay or contest
data.Description: Responding means either paying the fine or requesting a hearing; either sets RespondedOn and advances the citation to Responded.

data.Title: Rule enforced: R10 — Citation status is computed, never stored
data.Description: CitationStatus is re-derived on every read from raw facts and child events; there is no stored status column to drift.

data.Title: Rule enforced: R11 — No response by deadline is a default judgment
data.Description: If the response window passes with no RespondedOn, the citation moves to Adjudicated by default.

data.Title: Rule enforced: R12 — Contest status follows the latest hearing
data.Description: ContestStatus is derived from the newest related hearing: NotContested | HearingRequested → Scheduled → Heard.

data.Title: Rule enforced: R13 — Only a manager schedules a hearing
data.Description: Transitioning a hearing from HearingRequested to Scheduled is a manager-role action.

data.Title: Rule enforced: R20 — Fine is the violation's base fine
data.Description: The amount owed starts at the violation type's BaseFineUsd for the citation's jurisdiction.

data.Title: Rule enforced: R21 — Late payment incurs a jurisdiction penalty
data.Description: If payment is not made by the pay-by date, a penalty of LatePenaltyPct of the fine is added.

data.Title: Rule enforced: R22 — Prolonged non-payment goes to collections
data.Description: A Late citation becomes Collections after DaysLateToCollections days past the pay-by date.

data.Title: Rule enforced: R23 — A dismissed citation owes nothing
data.Description: If a hearing dismisses the citation, PaymentStatus is NotOwed regardless of the fine.

data.Title: Rule enforced: R30 — Active points roll up to the driver
data.Description: A driver's ActivePoints is the sum of points from adjudicated, non-dismissed citations.

data.Title: Rule enforced: R31 — Points drive license warning then suspension
data.Description: LicenseStatus is Warning at the jurisdiction's PointWarningThreshold and Suspended at PointSuspensionThreshold.

data.Title: Rule enforced: R40 — Regulations are data, not code
data.Description: Every regulatory knob — deadlines, fines, penalties, thresholds, school caps — is a column on Jurisdictions, editable without code changes.

data.Title: Rule enforced: R50 — Overdue work is urgent
data.Description: A work-queue item whose due date is in the past is urgent and sorts to the top.

data.Title: Rule enforced: R51 — Work items mirror their subject's deadline
data.Description: A work item's DueDate is carried from its subject (e.g. Citations.ResponseDueDate or PaymentDueDate).

data.Title: Rule enforced: R60 — Overrides require a reason
data.Description: Any OVERRIDE audit entry must carry a non-empty Reason; the trail is append-only.

data.Title: Rule enforced: R70 — Every assistant turn is costed
data.Description: Each assistant turn records input/output tokens and the exact pricing version used, so TotalCost is fully derivable.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-INTAKE-01 — Citation requires driver, violation, jurisdiction
data.Description: A citation must reference exactly one Driver, one ViolationType, and one issuing Jurisdiction; none may be null at intake.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-INTAKE-02 — Issue date present and not future-dated
data.Description: Every citation records an IssueDate that is on or before today; a future issue date is rejected at intake.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-INTAKE-03 — Driver response is pay-or-contest before the deadline
data.Description: The driver's response is one of {pay, contest} and must be recorded on or before the computed ResponseDeadline.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-DEADLINE-01 — Response deadline derives from jurisdiction DaysToRespond
data.Description: ResponseDeadline = IssueDate + the issuing jurisdiction's DaysToRespond; it recomputes whenever the rule changes.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-CITATION-01 — Citation status is derived, never stored
data.Description: CitationStatus (Issued -> Responded -> InContest -> Adjudicated -> Closed) is computed on read from facts and child CaseEvents; no stored status column is authoritative.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-CITATION-02 — Status order is monotonic
data.Description: A citation may only advance forward through its lifecycle states; backward transitions require an explicit, logged override.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-CONTEST-01 — Contesting requires a timely contest response
data.Description: The contest track only opens when DriverResponse = contest was recorded before the ResponseDeadline.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-CONTEST-02 — Contest track derives from the latest hearing
data.Description: ContestStatus (NotContested | HearingRequested -> Scheduled -> Heard) is derived from the most recent Hearings row for the citation.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-CONTEST-03 — Determination decides liability
data.Description: A hearing outcome of dismissed sets liability to none and zero fine owed; upheld confirms the original fine and points.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-HEARING-01 — Hearing requires a requested contest
data.Description: A Hearings row may only be created for a citation whose ContestStatus is HearingRequested or later.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-HEARING-02 — Scheduling is manager-or-admin only
data.Description: Only manager or admin roles may set a hearing's ScheduledDate; representatives request, they do not schedule.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-PAYMENT-01 — Payment track derives from payments and pay-by date
data.Description: PaymentStatus (Pending -> Due -> Late -> Collections | Paid | NotOwed) is computed from Payments rows and the jurisdiction pay-by date.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-PAYMENT-02 — Late penalty applies after the pay-by date
data.Description: Once the pay-by date passes with no full payment, the jurisdiction's LatePenaltyPercent is added to AmountOwed.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-PAYMENT-03 — Paid in full closes the case
data.Description: When recorded Payments equal AmountOwed, PaymentStatus becomes Paid and the citation advances toward Closed.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-FEE-01 — Base fine comes from the violation and jurisdiction
data.Description: The base fine is the ViolationType base amount adjusted by the jurisdiction's fee schedule; it is data, not a constant.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-LICENSE-01 — Active points roll up across a driver's citations
data.Description: A driver's ActivePoints is the sum of points from non-dismissed, non-expired citations under each jurisdiction's point window.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-LICENSE-02 — License track follows jurisdiction thresholds
data.Description: LicenseStatus (Valid -> Warning -> Suspended) is derived by comparing ActivePoints against the jurisdiction's warning and suspension thresholds.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-QUEUE-01 — Work queue is computed from deadlines and status
data.Description: WorkQueueItems are derived from overdue responses, due/late payments, and upcoming hearings, bucketed by urgency; nothing is hand-enqueued.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-AUDIT-01 — Every state-affecting change is logged
data.Description: Each status change, override, payment, and edit writes an append-only AuditLogEntries (and where lifecycle-relevant, a CaseEvents) row.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-AUDIT-02 — The log never contradicts the derived state
data.Description: The append-only history explains how current computed state was reached; it is additive narration, never an alternate source of truth.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-ASSISTANT-01 — Assistant answers are grounded in live data
data.Description: The assistant answers about a citation/rule/portal from live vw_* reads and the rulebook, recording each exchange as an AssistantTurns row.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-ASSISTANT-02 — Every turn is cost-accounted
data.Description: Each AssistantTurns row records input/output tokens and a computed cost via ModelPricingVersions for the turn's AiModel.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-RULES-01 — Jurisdiction knobs are editable data
data.Description: Deadlines, fines, penalty percentages, and point thresholds live in Jurisdictions/JurisdictionRules and every dependent value re-derives on edit.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-RULES-02 — Each jurisdiction rule cites its source
data.Description: A JurisdictionRules row links to the JurisdictionSourceDocuments / ReferenceDocuments authority that justifies it.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-RBAC-01 — Four canonical roles govern access
data.Description: Exactly four roles exist (admin, manager, representative, external-llm) and every permission decision references one of them.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-RBAC-02 — CRUD is per role, per table, per field
data.Description: What each role may create/read/update/delete is data at the table and field grain; the enforced API consults it on every operation.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-RBAC-03 — Row access follows per-role WHERE policies
data.Description: Each role-table pair carries a WHERE policy; a representative sees only their own citations while admin sees all, enforced on every vw_* read.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-RBAC-04 — Lower-trust roles get redacted projections
data.Description: Fields denied to a role (e.g. PII for external-llm) are stripped from responses at projection time, not nulled afterward.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-NAV-01 — Navigation is derived from PlatformNaviation rows
data.Description: The sidebar and route tree render from PlatformNaviation filtered by the current role's RoleVisibility; there is no hand-maintained menu.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-NAV-02 — Route order and pinning are data
data.Description: NavLevel, SortOrder, and PinToTop on PlatformNaviation determine ordering and which screens float to the top.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-AUTH-01 — Identity is resolved per request from the magic-link session
data.Description: Each request resolves to an AppUsers row and role via the MagicLinkConfig flow; identity feeds every RBAC and scoping decision.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-LICENSE-PKG-01 — Surfaces are gated by license/active flags
data.Description: Packages, features, tables, and routes carry IsLicensed/IsActive flags; unlicensed surfaces are revealed as upgrade prompts, not deleted.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-PLATFORM-01 — The rulebook JSON is the single source of truth
data.Description: All substrates (Postgres, Python, explain-dag, rulespeak) are mechanically derived from the rulebook by effortless build; edits go to the rulebook.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-PLATFORM-02 — The platform describes itself
data.Description: ERBTables/ERBFields/ERBFeatures/ERBPackages catalog every table, field, capability, and package so the app can render its own model.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-PLATFORM-03 — Build is generate + load; drift is guarded
data.Description: effortless build regenerates DDL/views/inserts and reloads the DB; a drift guard warns before a rebuild would clobber un-saved DB edits.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-GLOSSARY-01 — Domain terms are defined as data
data.Description: Every domain term used in a feature or rule is defined in GlossaryTerms under a GlossaryCategories group.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-DETERMINATION-01 — Determination is recorded
data.Description: A hearing determination (upheld / dismissed / reduced) must be recorded on the Citation before the citation-lifecycle may advance to adjudicated, and it writes a CaseEvents entry.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-DETERMINATION-02 — Dismissal reverses consequences
data.Description: When a determination dismisses a Citation, any assessed license points are reversed and any paid amount is refunded, restoring the Driver to make-whole state.

data.Title: Rule enforced: TT-STATE-01 — Lifecycle transitions are guarded
data.Description: Every state transition is gated by its guard condition and the firing role; the state-machine backbone rejects illegal transitions and logs each accepted one to CaseEvents.
